data.title: Accenture: Federal Contract Momentum and Embedded Policy Headwinds Support Buy Rating and Upside to $320 Target
data.description:
Analyst Kevin Mcveigh from UBS maintained a Buy rating on Accenture and keeping the price target at $320.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kevin Mcveigh has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Accenture’s strengthening federal business and attractive valuation. He highlights that Accenture Federal Services, which represents a meaningful slice of total revenue, is securing sizable, multi‑year contracts such as the Veterans Affairs health records modernization and the new National Weather Service platform, both of which enhance long‑term revenue visibility.

Mcveigh also notes that the company expects current DOGE‑related and government shutdown pressures to trough by fiscal Q4 2026, with these headwinds already embedded in guidance. At the same time, Accenture has steadily grown its share of the U.S. federal IT budget over the past decade, demonstrating competitive momentum, and the $320 price target based on a 19.5x 2028E EPS multiple suggests meaningful upside from current levels, supporting the Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Mcveigh is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 2.1% and a 51.36% success rate. Mcveigh covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Andersen Group, Inc. Class A, Robert Half, and Equifax.

In another report released on March 23, Mizuho Securities also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $280.00 price target.

data.title: CapitaLand Ascendas REIT: Accretive Strategic Acquisitions and Resilient Portfolio Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Lock Mun Yee of CGS International reiterated a Buy rating on CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, retaining the price target of S$3.21.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Lock Mun Yee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including the strategic acquisition of three income-generating assets in Singapore and Japan at a slight discount to their independent valuations. These properties come with long leases, high occupancy, and built-in rent escalations, which collectively enhance income visibility, lengthen portfolio WALE, and strengthen CapitaLand Ascendas REIT’s exposure to structurally growing sectors such as data centres and logistics.

In addition, the acquisitions are expected to be distribution per unit accretive even after factoring in a mix of equity and debt funding, with leverage rising only marginally and staying at a comfortable level. Lock Mun Yee also highlights the REIT’s diversified and resilient portfolio, visible growth from ongoing asset enhancement and redevelopment projects, and the potential for further accretive deals, which together support an unchanged target price and underpin the positive Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Mun Yee is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 4.3% and a 47.50% success rate. Mun Yee covers the Real Estate sector, focusing on stocks such as CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, UOL Group, and City Developments.

data.title: Merit Medical Systems: Leveraging Medtronic Partnership to Enter High-Growth Interventional Pain Market
data.description:
Merit Medical Systems, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Sam Eiber from BTIG maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $107.00 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sam Eiber has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Merit Medical Systems’ strategic positioning and growth prospects. He views the new exclusive distribution agreement with Medtronic for the FDA-cleared ViaVerte basivertebral nerve ablation system as a meaningful entry into a fast-expanding interventional pain segment, where procedure volumes and adoption are rising from a relatively small base.

He also notes that large competitors such as Boston Scientific and Stryker have recently committed substantial capital and product launches into this same therapeutic area, validating the market opportunity. Partnering with Medtronic leverages that company’s neuromodulation footprint and could help stabilize and grow Merit’s OEM business over time, supporting his positive long-term stance even without immediate changes to his financial model.

Eiber covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Establishment Labs Holdings, Merit Medical Systems, and Evolus. According to TipRanks, Eiber has an average return of -8.1% and a 18.18% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, TipRanks – xAI also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $75.00 price target.

data.title: KB Home: Near-Term Headwinds Temper Outlook Despite Build-to-Order Progress, Justifying Hold Rating
data.description:
Rafe Jadrosich, an analyst from Bank of America Securities, reiterated the Hold rating on KB Home. The associated price target was lowered to $56.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rafe Jadrosich has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including KB Home’s softer near‑term performance and outlook. First‑quarter earnings slightly missed expectations, full‑year delivery and revenue guidance was cut, and near‑term margins and SG&A leverage are set to deteriorate, leading him to reduce 2026 EPS forecasts and lower his price objective.

At the same time, he sees offsetting positives that support, but do not warrant exceeding, a Neutral stance. KB Home is progressing on its build‑to‑order strategy, which should lift margins in the second half, is shortening cycle times, and continues share repurchases, yet the stock’s discount to book value is viewed as appropriate given a sub‑7% projected ROE and a less favorable delivery backdrop.

In another report released today, TipRanks – Anthropic also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $56.00 price target.

KBH’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $62.380 to $52.940, which is a -15.13% drop .

data.description:

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Andrew Douglas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including RS Group’s ability to deliver profit slightly above market expectations despite a muted revenue backdrop. Management’s firm grip on costs, alongside lower-than-anticipated interest expenses, has enabled the company to protect earnings in a tougher trading environment.

data.title: ZEAL: Resilient Earnings, Growing Market Share, and Scalable Model Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Henrik Paganetty, an analyst from Jefferies, maintained the Buy rating on ZEAL Network. The associated price target was raised to €63.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Henrik Paganetty has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, notably ZEAL’s ability to deliver strong Q4 2025 results even in a weaker jackpot environment, with sales materially surpassing market expectations and EBITDA clearly outpacing consensus margins. He also highlights that the EBITDA margin remained robust despite a sharp drop in high-jackpot draws versus the prior year, underscoring the resilience and scalability of the business model.

At the same time, ZEAL modestly increased its already high online market share, confirming its solid positioning in the German digital lottery market and supporting confidence in sustained growth. The strong outperformance of the Traumhausverlosung format and the continued expansion and profitability of the Games segment, where revenue and EBITDA both rose markedly year on year, further underpin his positive view on future earnings and justify the Buy recommendation.
